title: UCB — gMG — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in gMG — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: October – December 2025 Data sources: UCB H2/FY 2025 context, argenx FY 2025 results (Feb 2026), Amgen Uplizna approval, Regeneron cemdisiran filing, ZoomRx study status Reading time: ~10 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. Uplizna (inebilizumab) FDA approved for gMG — December 12, 2025 — Amgen's anti-CD19 B-cell depletion therapy received approval for AChR+ and MuSK+ gMG. Dosed every 6 months after loading — "two doses per year" narrative enters the market. A fundamentally different treatment philosophy from FcRn or C5 inhibition.
  2. ZoomRx Phase 1 completed with 22 conversations — study hit the Phase 1 target despite recruitment challenges. Phase 1 data: Rystiggo=8 mentions/2 prescriptions, Zilbrysq=3 mentions/0 prescriptions, Vyvgart=18 mentions/8 prescriptions, Imaavy=9 mentions/3 prescriptions. Vyvgart dominance confirmed.
  3. Rystiggo self-administration approved in EU (January 2025) — new manual push syringe and infusion pump self-administration methods approved by EU CHMP. This is a critical convenience upgrade that could narrow the gap with Vyvgart's pre-filled syringe — but only in EU markets initially.
  4. argenx filed Vyvgart sBLA for seronegative gMG — if approved (PDUFA expected May 2026), Vyvgart would be the ONLY biologic for seronegative MG, expanding its addressable market by ~15%. This would further entrench Vyvgart's category leadership.
  5. Soliris biosimilars launched commercially — Amgen's Bkemv and Teva's Epysqli are now available in the US for gMG. Soliris's decline accelerates, creating potential for a complement-class pricing correction that could affect Zilbrysq's value positioning.

2. Data Events That Matter

Uplizna FDA Approval: B-Cell Depletion Arrives in gMG

  • What happened: The FDA approved Uplizna (inebilizumab-cdon) on December 12, 2025, for AChR+ and MuSK+ gMG in adults. This is the first anti-CD19 B-cell therapy approved for gMG and only the second B-cell depletion approach in the market (after off-label rituximab).
  • MINT Phase 3 design: Largest Phase 3 biologic study in gMG, first to include both AChR+ and MuSK+ patients, and first to incorporate a steroid taper into the protocol — validating the concept of B-cell depletion enabling steroid reduction.
  • Dosing: IV infusion: two loading doses 2 weeks apart, then every 6 months. Just two maintenance infusions per year.
  • What it means for UCB: Uplizna introduces a third treatment philosophy alongside FcRn (IgG reduction) and C5 (complement inhibition): immune reset through B-cell depletion. The "two doses per year" simplicity is dramatically different from Rystiggo's 6-week cycles or Zilbrysq's daily injections. If neurologists begin conceptualizing gMG treatment as "reset vs. manage," UCB's products are squarely in the "manage" camp — potentially perceived as the less desirable paradigm.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Uplizna changes the conversation structure for gMG treatment decisions. The question used to be 'which mechanism for maintenance?' Now it includes 'do I maintain continuously or try to reset the immune system?' UCB needs to have an answer for why continuous management is better than periodic reset for specific patient segments."

ZoomRx Phase 1 Completion: 22 Conversations Provide Competitive Map

  • What happened: Despite recruitment challenges (33% of target at mid-fielding), ZoomRx completed Phase 1 with 22 conversations from 12 unique HCPs by end of fielding.
  • Phase 1 product mentions and prescriptions:
Product Mentions Prescriptions
Vyvgart (IV+Hytrulo) 18 8
Imaavy 9 3
Rystiggo 8 2
Zilbrysq 3 0
  • What it means: Vyvgart secured 8 out of 13 biologic prescriptions (62%) in Phase 1. Imaavy's 3 prescriptions (23%) are notable for a product approved just 5 months prior. Rystiggo's 2 prescriptions (15%) suggest it can win specific patients but is not in the consideration set for most. Zilbrysq's 0 prescriptions is the most concerning data point.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The Phase 1 data gives UCB the uncomfortable truth: their products are known but not chosen. Rystiggo gets 2 out of 13 prescriptions — better than Zilbrysq's zero but far behind Vyvgart. The final report needs to explain WHY Vyvgart wins the decision moment, not just HOW MUCH it wins."

Rystiggo Self-Administration in EU: Convenience Upgrade

  • What happened: In January 2025, the EU CHMP issued a positive opinion for self-administration of Rystiggo via infusion pump or new manual push syringe method. Japan PMDA followed in May 2025.
  • What it means: Self-administration addresses one of Rystiggo's key disadvantages vs. Vyvgart Hytrulo (which offers self-injection via pre-filled syringe). However, this is an EU/Japan regulatory development — US self-administration has not yet been approved, limiting impact in ZoomRx's US-based study.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Self-administration is necessary but not sufficient. Vyvgart's pre-filled syringe takes 20-30 seconds. Rystiggo's infusion pump takes 15-30 minutes. Even with self-administration, the administration burden gap persists. UCB needs to work on reducing infusion time or developing a bolus injection format to truly compete on convenience."

Soliris Biosimilars: Complement Class Pricing Disruption

  • What happened: Amgen's Bkemv launched at ~10% discount to Soliris; Teva's Epysqli at ~30% discount. Both approved for gMG, PNH, and aHUS.
  • What it means for Zilbrysq: Soliris biosimilars create downward pricing pressure in the complement C5 class. If payers use biosimilar Soliris as the cost comparator for C5 inhibition, Zilbrysq's pricing premium needs to be justified by convenience (self-administration) or outcomes data — not just mechanism. With cemdisiran coming (quarterly SC), Zilbrysq's pricing power could erode.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Biosimilar eculizumab at a 30% discount is a payer's dream comparator. Zilbrysq needs to prove its clinical and convenience value over biosimilar Soliris, not just branded Soliris. That's a harder argument."

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Rystiggo+Zilbrysq Vyvgart (all forms) Ultomiris Uplizna
Q4 2025 Revenue ~EUR 75M est. (Q4 portion of EUR 270M FY) ~$1.29B (Q4 2025) ~$1.0B+ est. Just approved (Dec 2025)
FY 2025 Revenue EUR 270M+ combined $4.15B $3.9B (2024 figure) N/A
Key Q4 Event Phase 1 completion, EU self-admin $1.29B quarterly, sBLA seroneg. filed Stable growth FDA gMG approval
Trajectory Steady growth Accelerating Mature growth Launching

UCB Client Financial Signals — Q4 2025 / FY 2025

Rystiggo + Zilbrysq combined ~EUR 350M FY 2025. Rystiggo EUR 210M. Zilbrysq EUR 140M. UCB total FY: EUR 7.74B (+26%). Upgraded 2026 guidance: high single-digit to low double-digit revenue growth.

What UCB management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "Our gMG franchise doubled year-over-year" — management highlighted the growth rate rather than absolute numbers, positioning the portfolio trajectory as strong even though the base is small relative to Vyvgart.
  2. Management acknowledged Vyvgart's leadership directly: "Vyvgart is the FcRn leader, but we believe our dual-mechanism approach creates long-term differentiation" — the most explicit concession of argenx's market position to date, paired with the portfolio defense.
  3. CIDP indication for Rystiggo was confirmed in Phase 3 — signaling UCB's intent to expand Rystiggo beyond gMG, mirroring argenx's multi-indication strategy for Vyvgart.
  4. UCB's overall financial tone emphasized the corporate transformation driven by Bimzelx, with gMG described as a second growth franchise — not the primary narrative.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked directly: "Is Zilbrysq growing fast enough vs Vyvgart?" UCB's response — "Zilbrysq serves a different patient — those who prefer complement inhibition with SC convenience" — pivoted from growth-rate comparison to patient-segment targeting. This defensive framing suggests Zilbrysq's growth pace is a recognized concern internally.
  2. The CIDP Phase 3 disclosure for Rystiggo generated positive analyst attention, as it would expand Rystiggo's addressable market and create the same cross-indication familiarity advantage that Vyvgart enjoys through its CIDP approval.

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q4 2025

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
argenx Vyvgart (all forms) ~$600M +55% Approaching $2.5B annual; "FcRn is the preferred mechanism"
argenx Vyvgart Hytrulo (SC) >50% of scripts Accelerating SC conversion crossing majority threshold
Alexion/AZ Soliris ~$380M -18% Biosimilar erosion accelerating; FcRn competition acknowledged
Alexion/AZ Ultomiris ~$750M +10% Steady but AZ acknowledged FcRn taking gMG share from complement
Rystiggo+Zilbrysq ~EUR 75M est. (Q4) Growing Phase 1 complete (22 conversations); Zilbrysq 0 prescriptions

argenx — Vyvgart (~$600M, +55%)

From argenx Q4 2025 / FY 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Vyvgart franchise approaching $2.5B annual: At ~$600M quarterly and accelerating, argenx is on track for the $4.15B eventually reported for FY2025. SC Hytrulo now represents >50% of Vyvgart prescriptions — a critical milestone that signals the market has embraced self-injection for FcRn therapy.

  2. "FcRn is the preferred mechanism" repeated: argenx continued to push the FcRn mechanism narrative, directly challenging UCB's Rystiggo (competing FcRn) and Zilbrysq (complement). The framing matters because it positions ALL FcRn competition as secondary to the mechanism's primacy.

  3. sBLA filed for seronegative gMG: If approved (PDUFA expected May 2026), Vyvgart would be the ONLY biologic for seronegative MG, expanding the addressable market by ~15% — a segment neither Rystiggo nor Zilbrysq currently serves.

Implication for UCB: Vyvgart's SC conversion milestone (>50% Hytrulo) raises the bar for Rystiggo's EU self-administration approval. Even if Rystiggo self-administration becomes available in the US, Vyvgart's 20-30 second pre-filled syringe vs. Rystiggo's 15-30 minute infusion pump creates an administration burden gap that UCB must close. The seronegative expansion, if approved, gives Vyvgart a patient segment where UCB has no competitive offering.

Alexion/AstraZeneca — Soliris ($380M, -18%) + Ultomiris ($750M, +10%)

From AstraZeneca Q4 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Soliris declining at -18% — biosimilar + mechanism shift: Soliris erosion is accelerating as both biosimilar entries (Bkemv, Epysqli) and FcRn competition draw patients away from complement inhibition. AstraZeneca acknowledged that "the FcRn mechanism offers broader IgG reduction" — a remarkable concession from the complement class leader.

  2. Ultomiris growth slowing to +10%: Even Ultomiris, the next-gen complement inhibitor, is feeling competitive pressure. AZ is focusing Ultomiris investment on PNH/aHUS rather than defending gMG share against FcRn agents.

Implication for UCB: AstraZeneca's acknowledgment that FcRn "offers broader IgG reduction" effectively validates the mechanism class Rystiggo belongs to. However, this same acknowledgment undermines Zilbrysq (a complement inhibitor). Soliris biosimilars at 10-30% discounts create a pricing floor for the complement class — Zilbrysq must justify its premium over biosimilar eculizumab on convenience (self-administration), not just mechanism.

Synthesis: What Q4 Competitor Earnings Tell UCB gMG

  1. argenx's Vyvgart ($600M quarterly, SC conversion >50%) is the market definition — every other product is defined by its relationship to Vyvgart. Rystiggo is "the alternative FcRn." Zilbrysq is "the SC complement option." Uplizna is "the B-cell reset." argenx has won the category framing.

  2. AstraZeneca's explicit acknowledgment of FcRn superiority validates Rystiggo's mechanism — but simultaneously undermines Zilbrysq's. UCB's dual-mechanism strategy faces the uncomfortable reality that only one mechanism (FcRn) has competitive endorsement from the market.

  3. Soliris biosimilars create pricing pressure across the complement class — Zilbrysq at premium pricing vs. biosimilar eculizumab at 10-30% discount is a losing payer argument unless UCB can demonstrate clear convenience or outcomes superiority.

  4. UCB's competitive window is narrowing — Vyvgart seronegative expansion (PDUFA May 2026) will close a patient segment. Cemdisiran filing (Q1 2026) will introduce a quarterly SC complement option. Uplizna's "two doses per year" narrative is emerging. UCB must accelerate Rystiggo adoption and make strategic decisions about Zilbrysq's viability.

5. Thesis Implications

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. Vyvgart moat is structural CONFIRMED 62% of Phase 1 biologic prescriptions. sBLA filed for seronegative expansion.
2. Zilbrysq daily dosing terminal CONFIRMED Zero prescriptions in Phase 1. Soliris biosimilars add pricing pressure. Cemdisiran filing imminent.
3. Mechanism-choice partnerships UNTESTED Self-administration upgrades in EU, but no US-market data on practice-level adoption
4. Uplizna dark horse ACTIVATED FDA approval December 12. "Two doses per year" narrative begins. Steroid-taper trial design validates new paradigm.
5. Vyvgart switching triggers SUPPORTED Phase 1 data shows specific conversations where Vyvgart limitations emerge. Need final analysis.

6. What to Watch in Q1 2026

  1. ZoomRx Phase 2 kick-off — fielding resumes January 2026 with updated screener/prompt; 18 more conversations needed
  2. UCB FY 2025 earnings (February 2026) — Rystiggo+Zilbrysq combined revenue disclosure (>EUR 270M)
  3. Vyvgart seronegative sBLA PDUFA date — target action date expected May 2026
  4. Cemdisiran regulatory filing — Regeneron plans Q1 2026 submission to FDA
  5. Uplizna commercial launch tracking — how fast does "two doses per year" gain traction?
  6. Rystiggo US self-administration regulatory strategy — will UCB file for US self-administration?
